claims.claim_text: AgentOps keeps books, compiles context, and turns runs into evidence trails for a software factory.
claims.current_evidence: README public contract plus RPI/council/flywheel surfaces on disk.
claims.missing_proof: Claim remains capability-level until strict ledger and proof bundle validators are release gates.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Describe as repo-native bookkeeping and context compilation, not certified factory yield.

claims.claim_text: The flywheel can run autonomous improvement and overnight compounding loops.
claims.current_evidence: README documents /evolve and /dream surfaces; GOALS tracks automation gaps.
claims.missing_proof: Needs scheduled-run terminal artifacts and release posture gate before stronger autonomy wording.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Say bounded/scheduled compounding; do not imply unattended source mutation.

claims.claim_text: A first validated flow leaves evidence in .agents.
claims.current_evidence: RPI and validation flows emit local artifacts; Wave 0 canary checks proof paths.
claims.missing_proof: Needs tracked proof export path for public replay.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Use local evidence trail; avoid durable public proof claims until export exists.

claims.claim_text: ao evolve can run a bounded autonomous improvement cycle.
claims.current_evidence: CLI surface documented and exercised by local gates.
claims.missing_proof: Needs explicit release posture and rollback/demotion rule.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Bounded improvement cycle only; not autonomous merge or release.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps adds memory, pre-mortems, and validation across repeated sessions compared with session-local tools.
claims.current_evidence: Comparison docs and AgentOps memory/flywheel docs.
claims.missing_proof: Needs generated competitive freshness and live delta evidence for quantified superiority.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Frame as architectural differentiation, not measured throughput superiority.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps turns agent bookkeeping into typed, versioned, retrieved, validated engineering context.
claims.current_evidence: Product model maps to .agents artifacts, RPI packets, citations, and validation gates.
claims.missing_proof: Needs strict claim ledger and durable evidence bundle export.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Validated means gated locally unless L2/L3 artifacts are cited.

claims.claim_text: Factory-grade throughput is the aspiration, not a current generalized measured claim.
claims.current_evidence: Factory-yield ledger contract exists; observed pilot is blocked pending proof.
claims.missing_proof: Requires paired observed baseline/treatment yield artifacts and operator acceptance.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Say aspiration or pilot goal; do not claim generalized throughput.

claims.claim_text: /evolve performs bounded autonomous reconciliation against goals.
claims.current_evidence: GOALS and evolve docs describe the reconciliation loop.
claims.missing_proof: Needs release posture governance and demotion rules.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Bounded reconcile loop; no autonomous source mutation claim.

claims.claim_text: Mt. Olympus is framed as a forkable runtime proof, not the AgentOps product itself.
claims.current_evidence: Lineage section and related local artifacts.
claims.missing_proof: Needs externalized proof bundle before public evidence claims expand.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Call it lineage/runtime proof only with cited artifacts; do not sell it as current product proof.

claims.claim_text: The north-star path includes autonomous dream cycles and first validated flow onboarding.
claims.current_evidence: GOALS marks flywheel/dream targets and separate gates.
claims.missing_proof: Long-cycle corpus gates remain roadmap unless they run green in automation.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Keep as north star or gate target, not current enforced proof.

claims.claim_text: The eval workbench infrastructure is validated, but the live skill-on/off delta remains open.
claims.current_evidence: Workbench golden/broken and structural gates exist.
claims.missing_proof: Needs v2 live delta tasks and generated scorecards.
claims.closure_gate: make -C evals/workbench verify && make -C evals/workbench/v2 verify
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Publish generated zero/positive/negative deltas only from scorecards.

claims.claim_text: The docs index frames corpus continuity as typed, versioned, validated, and decay-ranked.
claims.current_evidence: Context lifecycle and flywheel docs describe the corpus path.
claims.missing_proof: Needs ledgered evidence levels for validated wording.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Validated refers to local gate posture unless evidence artifacts are cited.

claims.claim_text: Docs describe evolve as an autonomous improvement loop and RPI as a validated golden path.
claims.current_evidence: Evolve/RPI commands exist and are documented.
claims.missing_proof: Needs release governance before autonomy wording promotes beyond advisory.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Bounded autonomous cycles only; keep source mutation and merge authority human-gated.

claims.claim_text: By session 100, the repo carries validated patterns that future sessions can load.
claims.current_evidence: Flywheel, finding registry, and citation systems exist.
claims.missing_proof: Needs cross-session proof export and generated corpus metrics for the specific install.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Use as illustrative future-state unless backed by install-local corpus metrics.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps gives every session four product layers: Bookkeeping, Context Compiler, Validation Gates, Knowledge Flywheel.
claims.current_evidence: Brief doc and PRODUCT.md describe the four-layer model; assurance-profile.md maps layers to assurance roles.
claims.missing_proof: Layer architecture is product framing; per-layer validation evidence needed for 'validated' wording.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Architectural framing of the four product layers; per-layer proof claims need separate evidence.

claims.claim_text: The software-factory surface treats thin topics as discovery-only until evidence improves.
claims.current_evidence: Software-factory design rules and Wave 0 canary.
claims.missing_proof: Needs golden path proof bundle and release/profile governance.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Describe as design rule, not automated classifier until gate exists.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps brings high-assurance operating discipline to AI-agent-paced work without claiming certification.
claims.current_evidence: Assurance profile states boundaries and operator responsibilities.
claims.missing_proof: Needs evidence export, redaction, retention, and control-mapping bundles.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Posture only; no accreditation, classification, safety-critical, FedRAMP, or airworthiness claim.

claims.claim_text: The assurance profile maps the four compounding layers to assurance roles.
claims.current_evidence: Bookkeeping/context/validation/flywheel surfaces exist in docs and code.
claims.missing_proof: Needs claim-specific evidence export and source hash manifests.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Layer mapping is product architecture, not compliance proof.

claims.claim_text: Aerospace/IC-style discipline at AI-agent pace is a goal and posture, not current accreditation.
claims.current_evidence: Assurance profile documents operator boundaries and out-of-scope claims.
claims.missing_proof: Requires local authority control mapping and approval outside AgentOps.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Say engineered toward constrained environments; never say approved for them.

claims.claim_text: Factory yield must be observed as accepted, validated work per wall-clock hour, not template output.
claims.current_evidence: Factory yield ledger contract and schema exist.
claims.missing_proof: Needs observed paired baseline/treatment pilot data.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: No yield from templates, blocked admission, or manual_pending without operator acceptance.

claims.claim_text: Factory admission blocks source-mutating autonomous work before execution.
claims.current_evidence: Factory admission contract and daemon surfaces exist.
claims.missing_proof: Needs pilot harness proving blocked admission does not emit yield observations.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Admission is a control-plane stop, not proof of completed work.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps differentiates from Compound Engineer through automatic capture/scoring/injection and council validation.
claims.current_evidence: Comparison doc and AgentOps flywheel/council surfaces.
claims.missing_proof: Needs generated competitive freshness evidence and live task deltas.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Keep qualitative unless backed by generated scorecard numbers.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps differentiates from Superpowers through cross-session memory and validation beyond tests.
claims.current_evidence: Comparison doc and AgentOps memory/validation surfaces.
claims.missing_proof: Needs v2 workbench scorecards for measured value.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Qualitative comparison only; do not imply measured superiority.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps combines repo memory, scored promotion, citations, validation gates, and Dream/flywheel loops.
claims.current_evidence: Comparison RPI and AgentOps docs.
claims.missing_proof: Needs generated competitive freshness and proof export.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Architectural comparison, not benchmark claim.

claims.claim_text: Claude-Flow optimizes current throughput while AgentOps optimizes effectiveness over time.
claims.current_evidence: Comparison doc and AgentOps flywheel architecture.
claims.missing_proof: Needs measured throughput/effectiveness scorecards before quantitative claims.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Do not claim higher throughput without generated yield artifacts.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps holds a context-library / wiki-for-agents lane no competitor in the seven-competitor radar contests directly.
claims.current_evidence: Competitive radar table and lane segmentation in docs/comparisons/competitive-radar.md sourced from 2026-05-07 council research.
claims.missing_proof: Needs generated competitive-freshness scorecards and refreshed lane-segmentation evidence before quantified superiority wording.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Lane differentiation is structural framing, not measured outcome; do not imply benchmark-validated superiority.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps composes models per phase inside one RPI loop, distinct from cross-harness DRY parity.
claims.current_evidence: Comparison doc describes per-phase routing inside ao rpi; CLI surfaces exist for council and validation.
claims.missing_proof: Needs measured cross-model phase-routing artifacts and freshness scorecards before quantitative superiority wording.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Per-phase routing is a workflow capability claim, not a benchmark-validated speedup.

claims.claim_text: AgentOps adds a compounding corpus on top of skill inventory rather than a larger inventory of skills.
claims.current_evidence: Comparison doc and AgentOps .agents/ corpus surfaces produced by sessions.
claims.missing_proof: Needs generated corpus-growth and competitive-freshness scorecards before quantitative inventory-vs-corpus claims.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Corpus discipline is architectural framing, not measured throughput superiority over a marketplace.

claims.claim_text: Every artifact promotion needs identity, reproducibility, evaluation, evidence, and recovery; drop any one and trust collapses.
claims.current_evidence: Five-step primitive section in docs/trust-factory.md mapped to AgentOps mechanisms (identity → runs/, reproducibility → RPI + worktrees, evaluation → councils, evidence → citations + ratchets, recovery → rollback + learnings).
claims.missing_proof: Per-step evidence ledger linking each AgentOps mechanism to a measured outcome rather than a structural claim.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: The five-step primitive is a framing borrowed from established trust-factory disciplines; do not imply AgentOps invented it or has uniquely measured implementation.

claims.claim_text: .agents/ is markdown in your repo that agents read and contribute to, with maintenance compiled mechanically by the daemon rather than maintained voluntarily.
claims.current_evidence: wiki-for-agents.md describes .agents/ tree shape (council/, decisions/, findings/, learnings/, patterns/, planning-rules/, runs/) and the mechanical-maintenance contrast with Notion/Confluence.
claims.missing_proof: Quantified compounding-vs-bitrot comparison data (corpus size + decay-rank distribution over time) before turning the framing into a measured claim.
claims.anti_overclaim_wording: Mechanical maintenance is a design intent of the daemon, not yet a measured outcome; do not imply the daemon has been benchmarked against human-maintained wikis.
